metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_4825
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 5: Monitoring AMPA receptor lateral diffusion with quantum dot-based single-particle tracking showed that KCC2 (SLC12A5) suppression increases the lateral diffusion of GluR1 (GRIA1) in dendritic spines.

KCC2 extinction did not affect the exploratory behavior or confinement of slow receptors in spines (Fig. 5 A and C). KCC2 apparently constrains, directly or indirectly, lateral movements of rapid GluR1-containing AMPA receptors in dendritic spines. This effect seems to be independent of changes in spine head diameter, because control experiments showed that lateral diffusion of GluR1 was unrelated to spine size (Fig. S2). In contrast to its effect on GluR1 diffusion in spines, KCC2 suppression did not significantly alter diffusion on dendritic shafts.. Experimental description: M&M: "Hippocampal neurons were prepared from embryo day 19 Sprague-Dawley rat pups"
